author: @Oseois
text:
Hey Robert, I was watching the older videos on this and it reminded me of something I was researching a while back. I was really interested in playing around with some concrete foam, but every time I could find out what the surfactant was in the mixes, it was always some nasty, hard to find chemical.

Could you tweak this formula to make concrete foam?

I was also wondering if you think that graphene would help to strengthen concrete. 

Thanks!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i don't know mate - i have never tried and i suppose graphene could there have been quite a few research papers on it - though - again - i have never tried

replies.author: @Oseois
replies.text: Right on, thank you!

replies.author: @johnmcfadden9336
replies.text:
Oseois hi I've been researching about foamed cement lately and was wondering the same  I was thinking turning hemp fibres into Graphene fibres using rob method on making ultra sand  soaking the fibres in dilute sugar and water glass. 

I would imagine eco foam would work. There is a protein based foaming agent that sounds kosher that might suit you called green froth. 

Have you researched magnesium oxide cement and it's compatible to cellulose fibres and sawdust etc 
Basalt fibres are very promising  check out sudaglass fibre  very exciting

replies.author: @Oseois
replies.text: I will, thank you John!

replies.author: @Oseois
replies.text: Oh, and after reading up on that magnesium oxide cement, I would imagine that the nano cellulose would be superior to graphene, don't you think?

author: @World_Theory
text:
Does it clean oxides from metals? I recently took an old cheap flashlight (electric torch, to you, I guess) apart, and had to clean the blue-green copper oxide off the electrical contacts. I used some 620 grit sandpaper to take most of it off, then I polished it using the writing end if a graphite pencil, and a tissue to shine it up. Took a long time though. But I was fixing the thing for someone else, that liked the flashlight.

Anyway… A link in the description, to the old videos on this eco foam would be convenient.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
here is the playlist mate

https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLbQqm4rNo624vXg6twrK7A9ezpyLB2x4A

replies.author: @World_Theory
replies.text:
+Robert Murray-Smith Since you gave me the link to the playlist, I have gone and watched every video on it.

Now I wonder this: Is eco foam a good laundry soap, in comparison to the usual laundry soaps, for both price and effectiveness? If it can take rust stains out of cotton, I'll be mightily impressed.

One thing I have to note though, Is that I am weirdly particular about soap. I strongly dislike fabric softeners, and I hate it when my soap leaves stuff behind on my hands. I used foaming dish soap for my hands, and everyone in the house thinks I'm crazy.
